papers.title: RefineAnything: Multimodal Region-Specific Refinement for Perfect Local Details
papers.abstract: Abstract A multimodal diffusion-based model called RefineAnything is presented for region-specific image refinement that preserves backgrounds while enhancing local details, using a focus-and-refine strategy and boundary-aware loss functions.  AI-generated summary We introduce region-specific image refinement as a dedicated problem setting: given an input image and a user-specified region (e.g., a scribble mask or a bounding box), the goal is to restore fine-grained details while keeping all non-edited pixels strictly unchanged. Despite rapid progress in image generation, modern models still frequently suffer from local detail collapse (e.g., distorted text, logos, and thin structures). Existing instruction-driven editing models emphasize coarse-grained semantic edits and often either overlook subtle local defects or inadvertently change the background, especially when the region of interest occupies only a small portion of a fixed-resolution input. We present RefineAnything, a multimodal diffusion-based refinement model that supports both reference-based and reference-free refinement. Building on a counter-intuitive observation that crop-and-resize can substantially improve local reconstruction under a fixed VAE input resolution, we propose Focus-and-Refine, a region-focused refinement-and-paste-back strategy that improves refinement effectiveness and efficiency by reallocating the resolution budget to the target region, while a blended-mask paste-back guarantees strict background preservation. We further introduce a boundary-aware Boundary Consistency Loss to reduce seam artifacts and improve paste-back naturalness. To support this new setting, we construct Refine-30K (20K reference-based and 10K reference-free samples) and introduce RefineEval, a benchmark that evaluates both edited-region fidelity and background consistency. On RefineEval, RefineAnything achieves strong improvements over competitive baselines and near-perfect background preservation, establishing a practical solution for high-precision local refinement. Project Page: https://limuloo.github.io/RefineAnything/.

ru.title: Умное уточнение деталей изображения с гарантированным сохранением фона
ru.desc: Представлена RefineAnything — мультимодальная модель на основе диффузии для локального улучшения изображений с сохранением фона. Ключевой инновацией является стратегия Focus-and-Refine, которая перераспределяет разрешение в пользу целевой области через кропирование и ресайзинг, что значительно улучшает реконструкцию деталей. Модель поддерживает как reference-based, так и reference-free режимы уточнения, а специальная Boundary Consistency Loss уменьшает артефакты на границах при вставке обработанной области обратно. Для оценки качества авторы создали датасет Refine-30K и бенчмарк RefineEval, демонстрирующие превосходство метода в точном локальном улучшении при почти идеальном сохранении фона.

en.title: RefineAnything: Precision in Image Refinement with Background Preservation
en.desc: RefineAnything is a multimodal diffusion-based model designed for region-specific image refinement, focusing on enhancing local details while keeping the background intact. It introduces a focus-and-refine strategy that reallocates resolution to the specified region, improving the quality of local detail restoration. The model employs a boundary-aware loss function to minimize artifacts at the edges of the edited area, ensuring a seamless integration with the original background. With the creation of the Refine-30K dataset and the RefineEval benchmark, RefineAnything demonstrates significant advancements in maintaining fidelity in edited regions and background consistency compared to existing models.

zh.title: 区域特定图像细化的新突破
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为RefineAnything的多模态扩散模型，用于区域特定的图像细化。该模型采用聚焦与细化策略，利用边界感知损失函数，在增强局部细节的同时保持背景不变。尽管图像生成技术迅速发展，但现代模型仍常常面临局部细节崩溃的问题。RefineAnything通过区域聚焦的细化策略和严格的背景保护，提供了一种高精度的局部细化解决方案。

papers.title: Matrix-Game 3.0: Real-Time and Streaming Interactive World Model with Long-Horizon Memory
papers.abstract: Abstract Matrix-Game 3.0 enhances interactive video generation through memory-augmented diffusion models that achieve real-time 720p video synthesis with long-term temporal consistency.  AI-generated summary With the advancement of interactive video generation, diffusion models have increasingly demonstrated their potential as world models. However, existing approaches still struggle to simultaneously achieve memory-enabled long-term temporal consistency and high-resolution real-time generation, limiting their applicability in real-world scenarios. To address this, we present Matrix-Game 3.0, a memory-augmented interactive world model designed for 720p real-time longform video generation. Building upon Matrix-Game 2.0, we introduce systematic improvements across data, model, and inference. First, we develop an upgraded industrial-scale infinite data engine that integrates Unreal Engine-based synthetic data, large-scale automated collection from AAA games, and real-world video augmentation to produce high-quality Video-Pose-Action-Prompt quadruplet data at scale. Second, we propose a training framework for long-horizon consistency: by modeling prediction residuals and re-injecting imperfect generated frames during training, the base model learns self-correction; meanwhile, camera-aware memory retrieval and injection enable the base model to achieve long horizon spatiotemporal consistency. Third, we design a multi-segment autoregressive distillation strategy based on Distribution Matching Distillation (DMD), combined with model quantization and VAE decoder pruning, to achieve efficient real-time inference. Experimental results show that Matrix-Game 3.0 achieves up to 40 FPS real-time generation at 720p resolution with a 5B model, while maintaining stable memory consistency over minute-long sequences. Scaling up to a 2x14B model further improves generation quality, dynamics, and generalization. Our approach provides a practical pathway toward industrial-scale deployable world models.

ru.title: Памяти-ориентированная диффузия для реал-тайм синтеза видео высокого разрешения
ru.desc: Matrix-Game 3.0 представляет собой инновационную модель диффузии, дополненную механизмом памяти для интерактивной генерации видео. Модель решает задачу одновременного достижения высокого разрешения (720p) и реал-тайм синтеза видео при сохранении долгосрочной временной консистентности. Авторы улучшили качество данных через интеграцию синтетического контента, AAA-игр и реальных видео, разработали специальный фреймворк обучения с предсказанием остатков и извлечением из памяти. Для эффективного инференса применена многоэтапная дистилляция с использованием Distribution Matching Distillation, квантизации модели и оптимизации декодера VAE.

en.title: Revolutionizing Real-Time Interactive Video Generation
en.desc: Matrix-Game 3.0 introduces a new way to create interactive videos using advanced memory-augmented diffusion models. This method allows for real-time video generation at 720p resolution while ensuring that the videos maintain consistency over longer periods. The paper details improvements in data collection, model training, and inference techniques to enhance video quality and performance. Overall, it presents a significant step towards creating high-quality, long-form videos that can be used in various real-world applications.

zh.title: 增强记忆的交互式视频生成新突破
zh.desc: Matrix-Game 3.0 是一种增强记忆的交互式视频生成模型，能够实现720p实时视频合成，并保持长期的时间一致性。该模型通过改进数据、模型和推理框架，克服了现有方法在高分辨率和长时间一致性方面的局限。我们开发了一个工业级无限数据引擎，结合合成数据和真实视频增强，生成高质量的视频动作提示数据。实验结果表明，Matrix-Game 3.0 在720p分辨率下可实现每秒40帧的实时生成，同时在长达数分钟的序列中保持稳定的记忆一致性。

papers.title: CT-1: Vision-Language-Camera Models Transfer Spatial Reasoning Knowledge to Camera-Controllable Video Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract A Vision-Language-Camera model called CT-1 generates videos with accurate camera control by learning camera trajectories through diffusion transformers and wavelet regularization loss.  AI-generated summary Camera-controllable video generation aims to synthesize videos with flexible and physically plausible camera movements. However, existing methods either provide imprecise camera control from text prompts or rely on labor-intensive manual camera trajectory parameters, limiting their use in automated scenarios. To address these issues, we propose a novel Vision-Language-Camera model, termed CT-1 (Camera Transformer 1), a specialized model designed to transfer spatial reasoning knowledge to video generation by accurately estimating camera trajectories. Built upon vision-language modules and a Diffusion Transformer model, CT-1 employs a Wavelet-based Regularization Loss in the frequency domain to effectively learn complex camera trajectory distributions. These trajectories are integrated into a video diffusion model to enable spatially aware camera control that aligns with user intentions. To facilitate the training of CT-1, we design a dedicated data curation pipeline and construct CT-200K, a large-scale dataset containing over 47M frames. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework successfully bridges the gap between spatial reasoning and video synthesis, yielding faithful and high-quality camera-controllable videos and improving camera control accuracy by 25.7% over prior methods.

ru.title: Точное управление движением камеры в видеосинтезе через пространственное рассуждение
ru.desc: В работе представлена модель CT-1, которая объединяет vision-language понимание с генерацией видео под контролем камеры. Модель использует диффузионные трансформеры для точной оценки траекторий движения камеры на основе текстовых описаний и визуальной информации. Для эффективного обучения сложным распределениям траекторий применяется регуляризация в частотной области с использованием вейвлет-преобразования. Авторы создали большой датасет CT-200K с 47 миллионами кадров и достигли улучшения точности управления камерой на 25.7% по сравнению с существующими методами.

en.title: CT-1: Revolutionizing Video Generation with Smart Camera Control
en.desc: The paper introduces CT-1, a Vision-Language-Camera model that enhances video generation by enabling precise camera control through learned trajectories. It utilizes diffusion transformers and wavelet regularization loss to effectively capture complex camera movements, addressing limitations of previous methods that relied on manual input or provided inaccurate control. By integrating spatial reasoning into the video generation process, CT-1 allows for automated and user-aligned camera movements. The model is trained on a large dataset, CT-200K, which significantly improves camera control accuracy compared to existing techniques.

zh.title: CT-1：精确相机控制的视频生成新方法
zh.desc: CT-1是一种视觉-语言-相机模型，能够通过扩散变换器和小波正则化损失生成具有精确相机控制的视频。该模型解决了现有方法在文本提示下相机控制不精确或依赖手动参数的问题。CT-1通过学习复杂的相机轨迹分布，结合视觉-语言模块和扩散变换器，提升了视频生成的空间感知能力。实验结果表明，CT-1在相机控制精度上比之前的方法提高了25.7%。

papers.title: ELT: Elastic Looped Transformers for Visual Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Elastic Looped Transformers utilize recurrent transformer architecture with weight-sharing and intra-loop self-distillation to achieve parameter-efficient visual generation with adjustable computational cost and generation quality.  AI-generated summary We introduce Elastic Looped Transformers (ELT), a highly parameter-efficient class of visual generative models based on a recurrent transformer architecture. While conventional generative models rely on deep stacks of unique transformer layers, our approach employs iterative, weight-shared transformer blocks to drastically reduce parameter counts while maintaining high synthesis quality. To effectively train these models for image and video generation, we propose the idea of Intra-Loop Self Distillation (ILSD), where student configurations (intermediate loops) are distilled from the teacher configuration (maximum training loops) to ensure consistency across the model's depth in a single training step. Our framework yields a family of elastic models from a single training run, enabling Any-Time inference capability with dynamic trade-offs between computational cost and generation quality, with the same parameter count. ELT significantly shifts the efficiency frontier for visual synthesis. With 4times reduction in parameter count under iso-inference-compute settings, ELT achieves a competitive FID of 2.0 on class-conditional ImageNet 256 times 256 and FVD of 72.8 on class-conditional UCF-101.

ru.title: Параметрически эффективная генерация через переиспользуемые циклические трансформеры
ru.desc: В статье представлены Elastic Looped Transformers (ELT) — параметрически эффективные модели для генерации изображений и видео, основанные на архитектуре рекуррентного трансформера с переиспользованием весов. Для эффективного обучения предложен метод Intra-Loop Self Distillation (ILSD), при котором промежуточные конфигурации модели дистиллируются из полной конфигурации за один проход обучения. Из одного обучающего запуска получается семейство эластичных моделей с возможностью динамической регулировки компромисса между вычислительной стоимостью и качеством генерации. Модель достигает значительного сокращения параметров (в 4 раза) при сохранении конкурентных метрик качества (FID=2.0 на ImageNet и FVD=72.8 на UCF-101).

en.title: Efficient Visual Generation with Elastic Looped Transformers
en.desc: Elastic Looped Transformers (ELT) are a new type of visual generative model that uses a recurrent transformer architecture to be more efficient with parameters. Instead of using many unique transformer layers, ELT shares weights across iterative transformer blocks, which reduces the number of parameters needed while still producing high-quality images and videos. The model is trained using a technique called Intra-Loop Self Distillation (ILSD), where intermediate versions of the model learn from a more complex version to maintain quality throughout the training process. This approach allows for flexible inference, enabling users to adjust the balance between computational cost and output quality without changing the number of parameters.

zh.title: 弹性循环变换器：高效视觉生成的新方法
zh.desc: 弹性循环变换器（ELT）是一种高效的视觉生成模型，基于循环变换器架构。与传统生成模型依赖于深层独特的变换器层不同，我们的方法使用迭代的权重共享变换器块，显著减少参数数量，同时保持高质量的合成效果。为了有效训练这些模型进行图像和视频生成，我们提出了内部循环自蒸馏（ILSD）的概念，通过从教师配置中提取学生配置，确保模型深度的一致性。ELT在参数数量相同的情况下，实现了计算成本和生成质量之间的动态权衡，显著提高了视觉合成的效率。

papers.title: VisionFoundry: Teaching VLMs Visual Perception with Synthetic Images
papers.abstract: Abstract VisionFoundry generates synthetic visual question answering data using large language models and text-to-image prompts to improve visual perception tasks in vision-language models.  AI-generated summary Vision-language models (VLMs) still struggle with visual perception tasks such as spatial understanding and viewpoint recognition. One plausible contributing factor is that natural image datasets provide limited supervision for low-level visual skills. This motivates a practical question: can targeted synthetic supervision, generated from only a task keyword such as Depth Order, address these weaknesses? To investigate this question, we introduce VisionFoundry, a task-aware synthetic data generation pipeline that takes only the task name as input and uses large language models (LLMs) to generate questions, answers, and text-to-image (T2I) prompts, then synthesizes images with T2I models and verifies consistency with a proprietary VLM, requiring no reference images or human annotation. Using VisionFoundry, we construct VisionFoundry-10K, a synthetic visual question answering (VQA) dataset containing 10k image-question-answer triples spanning 10 tasks. Models trained on VisionFoundry-10K achieve substantial improvements on visual perception benchmarks: +7% on MMVP and +10% on CV-Bench-3D, while preserving broader capabilities and showing favorable scaling behavior as data size increases. Our results suggest that limited task-targeted supervision is an important contributor to this bottleneck and that synthetic supervision is a promising path toward more systematic training for VLMs.

ru.title: Фабрика синтетических данных для обучения зрительного восприятия
ru.desc: VisionFoundry — это система для автоматического создания синтетических данных для обучения vision-language моделей (VLM). Система использует большие языковые модели (LLM) для генерации вопросов, ответов и текстовых описаний, которые затем преобразуются в изображения с помощью text-to-image моделей. Авторы создали датасет VisionFoundry-10K с 10 тысячами синтетических примеров для 10 различных задач визуального восприятия, таких как понимание глубины и распознавание точки зрения. Обучение на этом датасете значительно улучшает производительность VLM на бенчмарках, что демонстрирует эффективность целевой синтетической информации для восполнения пробелов в обучающих данных.

en.title: Empowering Vision-Language Models with Synthetic Supervision
en.desc: VisionFoundry is a synthetic data generation pipeline designed to enhance visual question answering (VQA) in vision-language models (VLMs). It utilizes large language models (LLMs) to create questions, answers, and text-to-image prompts based solely on task keywords, generating images without needing reference images or human input. The resulting dataset, VisionFoundry-10K, consists of 10,000 image-question-answer triples across various tasks, leading to significant performance improvements in visual perception benchmarks. This approach highlights the potential of targeted synthetic supervision to address the limitations of traditional natural image datasets in training VLMs.

zh.title: 合成监督助力视觉语言模型提升
zh.desc: VisionFoundry 是一个合成视觉问答数据生成系统，利用大型语言模型和文本到图像的提示来提升视觉语言模型在视觉感知任务中的表现。现有的视觉语言模型在空间理解和视角识别等低级视觉技能上仍然存在困难，部分原因是自然图像数据集对这些技能的监督有限。VisionFoundry 通过仅输入任务名称，生成问题、答案和文本到图像的提示，并合成图像，验证与专有视觉语言模型的一致性，从而提供有针对性的合成监督。通过构建 VisionFoundry-10K 数据集，模型在视觉感知基准测试中取得了显著提升，表明合成监督是提升视觉语言模型训练的有效途径。
